frames: The agent begins in an upright posture, with its lower segment starting to tilt slightly to the right.
The lower segment continues its rightward tilt, and the upper segment starts to lean, maintaining a relatively straight overall form.
The agent is significantly angled to the right, with its lower segment approaching a horizontal position.
The agent's lower segment makes contact with the ground, and the upper segment is sharply angled upwards and to the right.
The lower segment is now fully on the ground, and the upper segment bends at the joint, moving towards a more vertical alignment.
The agent forms an 'L' shape, with the lower segment flat on the ground and the upper segment nearly perpendicular to it.
The upper segment begins to tilt back to the right, and the lower segment starts to lift off the ground.
The agent is again significantly tilted right, with both segments angled upwards from the ground, appearing to swing back.
